_notes: X2-U1 WORLD-grounded fixture. Block U (unpredictable re-need of EVICTED lineage): the DEP0033 status per the CURRENT RULING -> answer 'revoked', answerable only by dep0033-revoked, which C pruned during block P and must rematerialize. B (no recovery) cannot get it back and answers 'deprecated' -> B fails on the round-trip record, proving C's recovery necessary. Same record universe as deprecated.json; parallel status records, questions key on 'current ruling' (U) vs 'original filing' (P), never the answer words.
question: Per the DEP0033 current ruling, what is the status of the module-level events.listenerCount()? Answer in one word.

oracle_ref.representativeness: Node DEP0033 status after the v24.14.0/v25.4.0 revocation; real version-controlled fact, source != authored.

records.text: Unrelated Node deprecations archive: assorted DEP0XXX notices covering legacy Buffer constructors, the domain module, _linklist, several crypto cipher defaults, the punycode bundle, url query handling, util.isArray and related type-check helpers, and documentation-only migration notices consolidated across the v4 through v12 release lines.
DEP0033 original filing, Node v3.2.0: the module-level events.listenerCount() is deprecated.
DEP0033 current ruling, Node v24.14.0: the module-level events.listenerCount() is revoked.
